project_name: GB10 Joule Router Live Calibration Adapter

decision.recommended_next_action: Optionally repeat the live run with a Qwen3-4B vLLM endpoint and synchronized nvidia-smi CSV sampling to remove the current model/backend calibration mismatch.
decision.stop_reason: The adapter calibrated from sibling GB10 power traces, provisioned a real GB10 llama.cpp OpenAI-compatible endpoint, completed live chat shadow-routing, and passed regression tests.

recommended_next_action: Optionally repeat the live run with a Qwen3-4B vLLM endpoint and synchronized nvidia-smi CSV sampling to remove the current model/backend calibration mismatch.
run_notes_tail: - Implemented `src/gb10_live_calibration_adapter.py`, a dependency-light calibration + shadow-routing adapter for vLLM/llama.cpp/OpenAI-compatible local endpoints.

- Adapter supports sibling summary/GPU CSV discovery, two observed GB10 power CSV formats, idle-floor-subtracted power integration, calibrated joules/token constants, `/v1/models` or `/models` plus `/v1/chat/completions` or `/chat/completions` probes, and dry-run/live shadow decisions.
- Added regression tests for power parsing/integration, calibration, and shadow policy behavior.
- Created `artifacts/sibling_traces/manifest.json` pointing to the sibling vLLM summaries/GPU traces used for calibration.

- Ran calibration-only shadow benchmark: `results/gb10_live_calibration_adapter.json` uses 8 sibling vLLM/GB10 runs, model hint `Qwen/Qwen3-4B`, idle power ~21.97 W, active base ~7.36 J/request, prompt ~0.221 J/token, completion ~0.250 J/token, p95 latency ~7.41 s; 3 dry-run shadow requests completed with estimated total 223.62 J.

- Probed likely local-serving ports from sibling artifacts and common defaults via `results/gb10_live_calibration_with_probe_attempts.json`: parent vLLM ports 8240 and 8150 were refused; port 8000 is a non-OpenAI health service; port 8080 is an HTML web UI, not JSON OpenAI-compatible.
- Verification: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> 6 passed; `.venv/bin/python src/gb10_live_calibration_adapter.py --out results/gb10_live_calibration_adapter.json` completed.
## Current interpretation
- The branch now has concrete executable work and calibrated GB10 energy estimates from real sibling GPU traces, so the successor mechanism materially advanced beyond the synthetic parent MVP.

- Live shadow routing is implemented and probe-gated, but no currently running local OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint was found in this turn; the next concrete step is to start/provision a vLLM/llama.cpp/Transformers endpoint from cached or downloadable weights and rerun with `--probe --live`.
## 2026-04-17 second execution plan
- Re-check the branch adapter/test state and local serving availability instead of assuming the prior probe result is still current.
- Search cached model locations and installed serving libraries for a viable local OpenAI-compatible endpoint path.

- If no vLLM/llama.cpp endpoint is already live, run a bounded project-local OpenAI-compatible shim or documented setup attempt, verify `/models` and `/chat/completions`, then rerun the adapter with `--probe --live`.
- Persist live/probe artifacts, run tests, and update the decision artifact with the observed evidence.
## 2026-04-17 live GB10 llama.cpp shadow-routing execution

- Found reusable GB10-capable llama.cpp builds at `/home/jeremy/projects/nemotron-super/llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-server` and cached GGUF models including `/home/jeremy/projects/enoch_testing_ground/projects/source-record-redacted/models/Qwen3-0.6B-Q4_K_M.gguf`.

- Started `llama-server` from neutral directory `/tmp/gb10_joule_router_service` on `127.0.0.1:18240` with GPU offload (`-ngl 99`) and model alias `Qwen3-0.6B-Q4_K_M`; verified it reported CUDA device `NVIDIA GB10` and served OpenAI-compatible `/v1/models` plus `/v1/chat/completions`.

- Ran live adapter shadow benchmark: `results/gb10_live_llama_cpp_shadow.json`. Primary live llama.cpp endpoint completed all 3 sample chat requests; actual latencies were about 0.324 s, 0.509 s, and 0.471 s. A non-live calibrated reference endpoint was intentionally present and probe-gated as unavailable.

- Ran a second live shadow-policy validation with two probe-passing endpoint specs backed by the same live llama.cpp server but different quality metadata: `results/gb10_live_llama_cpp_quality_shadow.json`. Both specs passed `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions`; all 3 live requests succeeded; the router shadow policy changed route on 2/3 requests (66.7%) when quality metadata made the primary insufficient.

- Captured point-in-time GB10 `nvidia-smi` evidence before/after the first live run in `artifacts/live_llama_cpp/nvidia_smi_before.csv` and `artifacts/live_llama_cpp/nvidia_smi_after.csv` (power rose from ~12.16 W idle to ~34.00 W / 88% GPU util after live requests).
- Stopped the helper llama.cpp server before yielding; port 18240 is no longer listening.
- Verification: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q` -> 6 passed.
## Current interpretation after live run

- The branch now satisfies the core successor objective: sibling GB10/vLLM power traces calibrate the energy profile, and a real GB10-backed llama.cpp OpenAI-compatible endpoint was provisioned and used for live shadow-routing requests.

- Evidence is positive but model-mismatched: live serving used cached Qwen3-0.6B GGUF, while the strongest sibling calibration traces are Qwen3-4B vLLM runs. The next improvement would be a synchronized live power CSV around the live llama.cpp/vLLM benchmark or a Qwen3-4B endpoint, not another routing branch.
